related_work_text:
Related Work
Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Multi-hop
QA. Multi-hop QA requires multi-step reasoning
over evidence from multiple sources (Li and Du,
2023; Zhang et al., 2024; Cheng et al., 2025).
Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) addresses
this challenge by coupling retrieval with genera-
tion (Ji et al., 2023; Feng et al., 2024; Shi et al.,
2024). Existing RAG methods for multi-hop QA
mainly fall into two categories. Graph-structured
approaches (Gutiérrez et al., 2025; Gutiérrez et al.,
2025; Fang et al., 2025) organize textual knowledge
into entity–relation graphs and aggregate evidence
along multi-hop paths, but require explicit graph
construction and maintenance (Gutiérrez et al.,
2025; Gutiérrez et al., 2025). Iterative RAG ap-
proaches (Trivedi et al., 2023; Shao et al., 2023;
Liu et al., 2024; Jiang et al., 2025; Li et al., 2025)
perform retrieval and reasoning in multiple steps
(e.g., IRCOT (Trivedi et al., 2023), Search-o1 (Li
et al., 2025)), yet heavily rely on LLM-generated
intermediate thoughts and are thus prone to halluci-
nation (Liu et al., 2024; Jiang et al., 2025). In con-
trast, GlobalRAG leverages reinforcement learning
to enhance global planning and coordinated reason-
ing, enabling more accurate evidence localization
and integration for multi-hop QA.
Large Language Models and Reinforcement
Learning.
Reinforcement learning has sub-
stantially improved complex reasoning and self-
verification in LLM post-training (OpenAI et al.,
2024; Guo et al., 2025; Dong et al., 2025; Lai
et al., 2025), and has recently been extended to
RAG (Jin et al., 2025a; Chen et al., 2025; Song
et al., 2025a; Wang et al., 2025). Search-R1 (Jin
et al., 2025a) adopts outcome-based rewards to en-
courage retriever usage, while StepSearch (Wang
et al., 2025) scores per-step retrieval quality via in-
formation gain and redundancy penalties. However,
lacking explicit global planning and suffering from
unfaithful execution, these methods remain subop-
timal for multi-hop QA (Jin et al., 2025a; Wang
et al., 2025). By contrast, we introduce a Plan-
ning Quality Reward and a Subgoal Completion
Reward to explicitly reinforce global reasoning and
structured progress in multi-hop QA.
3
